content.problemStatement:
You are given an undirected graph GGG with NNN nodes and MMM edges. Each edge eee has a color cec_ece‚Äã. Define the cost of a path as the number of times the edge color changes.

Formally, if the path consists of edges e1,e2,‚ãØ‚Äâ,eke_1, e_2, \cdots, e_ke1‚Äã,e2‚Äã,‚ãØ,ek‚Äã in this order, the cost of the path is the number of 1‚â§i<k1 \leq i < k1‚â§i<k, such that cei‚âÝcei+1c_{e_i} \neq c_{e_{i+1}}cei‚Äã‚ÄãÓÄÝ=cei+1‚Äã‚Äã.

You are given two nodes sss and ttt. Find the minimum cost of a path from sss to ttt. The path is allowed to have cycles.

Please note that there **might** be multiple edges between two nodes but there are no self loops.


Input Format

The first line contains NNN and MMM, the number of nodes and edges in the graph respectively.
Each of the next MMM lines contain three integers, u,v,cu, v, cu,v,c denoting an undirected edge between uuu and vvv having color ccc.
The last line contains two integers, sss and ttt.


Output Format
Print the minimum cost (as defined in the statement) of a path from sss to ttt in the graph.
constraints: There will be at least 1 path from sss to ttt

We choose the path 1‚Üí2‚Üí4‚Üí51 \rightarrow 2 \rightarrow 4 \rightarrow 51‚Üí2‚Üí4‚Üí5. The sequence of edge colors is 1,2,21, 2, 21,2,2. Clearly, we change color only once, and that is the answer.
